edges.description: Alpha-2C-AR activation is potentiated by RhoA/ROCK signaling.

nodes.description: Cold triggers alpha-2C adrenoceptor (ADRA2C) activation in microvascular smooth muscle cells, mediating local cooling-induced vasoconstriction of digital arteries and arterioles. Alpha-2C-AR is the sole mediator of cold-induced vasoconstriction in the peripheral vasculature.

evidence.snippet: alterations in activity of the peripheral adrenoceptor have been implicated, specifically an enhanced smooth muscle contraction due to overexpression or hyperactivity of postsynaptic alpha 2 receptors

evidence.reference_title: Cold responses and hormonal echoes: a comprehensive view of Raynaud's vascular dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: estrogen plays a fundamental role in potentiating the expression and function of α2C adrenoceptor (α2C-AR), the sole mediator of local cooling-induced vasoconstriction

nodes.description: RhoA/ROCK signaling potentiates alpha-2C adrenoceptor-mediated vasoconstriction and reactive oxygen species generation in vascular smooth muscle cells, amplifying the vasospastic response to cold.

evidence.reference_title: Raynaud's Phenomenon: A Current Update on Pathogenesis, Diagnostic Workup, and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: Raynaud's phenomenon (RP) is a condition characterized by episodic, excessive vasoconstriction in the fingers and toes, triggered by cold or stress.
